config.system_prompt:
You are a reviewer writing comprehensive integration and E2E tests for the cascade delete feature. Your goal is to verify all six acceptance criteria are met and the system handles failure cases gracefully.

Test structure:
- Integration tests interact with real (or localstack) DynamoDB and S3, mocking only Bedrock KB calls
- E2E tests exercise full lifecycle: create resource → populate child data → DELETE → assert all data gone
- Idempotency: verify second DELETE on same mission/project is a no-op (no errors raised)
- Partial failure recovery: use mock.side_effect to simulate S3 failure on first call, then verify retry deletes remaining resources
- OCXP preservation: assert _delete_ocxp_project still called as background task after project delete

Acceptance criteria to verify:
- AC1: list_objects_v2 for mission S3 prefix returns empty after delete
- AC2: DynamoDB queries for workflows/memos/WeaverPlans by mission_id return empty after delete
- AC3: Bedrock KB delete_from_kb() called with correct document references for mission content
- AC4: All child missions satisfy AC1-AC3 after project delete
- AC5: Project-level S3 prefixes (project/ and context/) empty after project delete
- AC6: _delete_ocxp_project() still executes on project delete

Edge cases to cover in tests:
- Mission with no child resources (fast path)
- Mission with thousands of S3 objects (pagination)
- Partial failure: S3 deletes succeed, DynamoDB fails
- Partial failure: child DynamoDB records deleted, mission record delete fails
- Project with zero child missions
- Concurrent delete requests for same mission (idempotency)
- Bedrock KB document discovery fails

Final code review: verify all new code follows Python typing conventions, all methods have docstrings, no bare except clauses, structured logging uses consistent field names across all files.

behavior: 1. Define a module-level pytest fixture `mission_with_resources` that: (a) creates a mission DynamoDB record in localstack, (b) uploads at least 3 S3 objects under the prefix workspaces/{workspace}/context/{mission_id}/, (c) inserts workflow records into the contexthub-workflows table with the mission_id, (d) inserts memo records into the contexthub-memos table with the mission_id, (e) inserts WeaverPlan records into the contexthub-mission-metadata table with sort keys matching WEAVER_PLAN#v{version}#{mission_id}, and (f) returns the mission_id and workspace for use in tests.

2. In `test_mission_delete_cleans_s3`: call DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id} via http_client and assert HTTP 200; then call localstack S3 list_objects_v2 with the prefix workspaces/{workspace}/context/{mission_id}/ and assert the response KeyCount is 0 (AC1). This verifies that the modified delete_mission router endpoint correctly dispatches the cascade background task.

3. In `test_mission_delete_cleans_dynamodb_children`: call DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id} via http_client and assert HTTP 200; then query the contexthub-workflows table by mission_id and assert 0 items returned (verifying WorkflowRepository.delete_by_mission executed); query the contexthub-memos table by mission_id and assert 0 items (verifying MemoRepository.delete_by_mission executed); query the contexthub-mission-metadata table for WEAVER_PLAN# sort key prefix and assert 0 items (verifying WeaverPlanRepository.delete_by_mission executed); all three assertions are required to pass (AC2).

4. In `test_mission_delete_cleans_kb`: patch ContextService.delete_from_kb before issuing DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id}; assert HTTP 200; assert delete_from_kb was called at least once; assert each call argument contains DeleteFromKBDocument objects whose source_uri fields match the S3 URIs of the mission's S3 objects (AC3).
5. Each test function is independent and uses the `mission_with_resources` fixture with function scope so a fresh mission is created for every test.
6. Use pytest.mark.integration to tag all tests in this module so the CI pipeline can selectively run integration tests.
7. Include a module-level conftest or fixture that configures the FastAPI app dependency overrides to inject localstack-backed repository instances and the mocked ContextService.

spec.name: test_cascade_delete_edge_cases
spec.summary: Integration test module covering idempotency of repeated deletes and partial-failure recovery scenarios for the cascade delete service. Verifies that a second DELETE on an already-deleted mission is a no-op, that S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix raising ClientError does not abort DynamoDB cleanup via CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_mission, and that a subsequent delete completes remaining cleanup.
inputs: mission_id: str -- UUID of the mission created during fixture setup
workspace: str -- tenant isolation scope
http_client: TestClient -- FastAPI test client for issuing DELETE requests
localstack_s3: boto3.client -- S3 client for pre/post assertions and fault injection
localstack_dynamodb: boto3.resource -- DynamoDB resource for state assertions
mock_s3_delete: MagicMock -- mock of S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix with side_effect to simulate S3 ClientError on first call
caplog: pytest.LogCaptureFixture -- for asserting structured log fields on error paths
outputs: pytest pass/fail per test function
log capture assertions verifying error is logged with fields: workspace, entity_id, resource_type
second-call assertions verifying remaining cleanup completes on retry
behavior: 1. In `test_idempotent_double_delete`: use `mission_with_resources` fixture; call DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id} the first time and assert HTTP 200; call DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id} a second time with the same mission_id and assert HTTP 200 (no exception, no 404, no 500); verify that no exception is raised and no error is logged on the second call; verify final storage state is clean (S3 prefix empty, DynamoDB records gone). This directly tests CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_mission idempotency.

2. In `test_partial_failure_s3_raises_dynamodb_cleanup_continues`: before issuing DELETE, patch S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix to raise botocore.exceptions.ClientError on the first call; issue DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id} and assert HTTP 200 (cascade errors must not surface to HTTP response); assert the S3 ClientError is logged with structured fields (workspace, entity_id, resource_type='s3'); assert that DynamoDB cleanup still ran -- query workflows, memos, WeaverPlan tables and assert all records are gone; assert that the mission DynamoDB record itself is gone. This verifies CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_mission continues after S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix failure.

3. In `test_partial_failure_recovery_retry`: use a fixture where S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix raises ClientError only on the first call (side_effect=[ClientError, DEFAULT]); issue first DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id} -- expect HTTP 200 with errors logged; verify S3 objects still present (first call failed) but DynamoDB records are gone; issue second DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id} -- expect HTTP 200; verify S3 prefix is now empty (second call succeeded).

4. In `test_partial_failure_dynamo_after_child_records_deleted`: patch MissionRepository.delete_mission to raise ClientError only on the final mission record delete (after workflows/memos/WeaverPlans are already deleted); issue DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id}; assert HTTP 200; assert workflows/memos/WeaverPlan records are gone from DynamoDB; assert error is logged for the mission record delete failure; issue second DELETE -- assert HTTP 200 and mission record eventually gone (idempotent recovery). This tests the step ordering guarantee in CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_mission.
5. Each test uses function-scoped fixtures for full isolation.
6. Tag all tests with pytest.mark.integration.
constraints: MUST assert HTTP 200 on both first and second DELETE calls for idempotency tests -- CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_mission MUST be safe to call multiple times for the same entity without raising errors

MUST verify that individual step failures (S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix ClientError) are logged and collected but do NOT stop the cascade -- per CascadeDeleteService spec: 'Individual step failures logged and collected but do not stop cascade'
MUST verify the structured log contains consistent field names: workspace, entity_id, resource_type, count_deleted as required by the code review criteria in P3.5
MUST verify that mission DynamoDB record deletion (step 6 in cascade) executes last -- partial failure recovery depends on re-entrant cleanup finding child records intact
MUST NOT use bare except clauses in test helper code -- catch specific exceptions (botocore.exceptions.ClientError) in fault injection side_effects
MUST use mock.side_effect with a list [ClientError(...), DEFAULT] pattern to simulate failure only on first call, allowing retry to succeed
MUST patch S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix specifically (not boto3.client.delete_objects) to respect the abstraction boundary between CascadeDeleteService and S3Repository

DECISION: Individual step failures logged and collected but do not stop cascade -- test must assert cascade_delete_mission continues to DynamoDB cleanup even when S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix raises ClientError

DECISION: Step 6 (mission record delete) MUST execute last to preserve re-entrant recovery capability -- partial failure test must verify this ordering by checking DynamoDB child records are gone but mission record remains when step 6 fails
edge_cases: Second DELETE on a mission where the mission record no longer exists in DynamoDB -- cascade_delete_mission must return a no-op CascadeDeleteResult with zero counts and no errors
S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix raises ClientError during list_objects_v2 (not just delete_objects) -- verify this is also caught, logged, and cascade continues
All three DynamoDB cleanup steps fail -- verify all three errors are collected in CascadeDeleteResult.errors list and logged separately
Mission with no resources at all (clean state) -- second delete on a mission that had no S3/DynamoDB children; verify no errors logged
S3 ClientError on the second page of a paginated list (ContinuationToken present) -- verify error is caught mid-pagination and logged with correct prefix context

spec.name: test_cascade_delete_e2e
spec.summary: End-to-end test module exercising the full cascade delete lifecycle against a running API: create resources, issue DELETE, assert all data removed across S3, DynamoDB, and Bedrock KB planes. Covers AC1-AC6 holistically plus partial failure recovery in three named E2E scenarios. Exercises CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_mission and CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_project through their respective modified router endpoints.
inputs: http_client: TestClient or httpx.AsyncClient -- full FastAPI application client, not mocked at the router level
workspace: str -- isolated E2E test workspace to prevent data contamination
localstack_s3: boto3.client -- S3 client for full-lifecycle assertions
localstack_dynamodb: boto3.resource -- DynamoDB resource for full-lifecycle assertions
mock_bedrock_kb: MagicMock -- mock of ContextService.delete_from_kb (Bedrock not available in localstack)
mock_delete_ocxp_project: MagicMock -- mock of _delete_ocxp_project for AC6 verification in project scenario
outputs: pytest pass/fail per E2E scenario
comprehensive assertions across all three data planes (S3, DynamoDB, Bedrock KB) verifying AC1-AC6
timing assertions where appropriate to verify cascade completes within acceptable duration
behavior: 1. Define E2E fixture `full_mission_resource_set` that creates via the API (not direct DB calls): a mission record, S3 files uploaded under workspaces/{workspace}/context/{mission_id}/ (minimum 5 files), workflow records linked to mission_id, memo records linked to mission_id, WeaverPlan records with sort keys WEAVER_PLAN#v1#{mission_id} and WEAVER_PLAN#v2#{mission_id}, and KB document metadata (mocked); return mission_id and all created resource identifiers.

2. In `test_e2e_mission_with_full_resource_set` (AC1-AC3): issue DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id} via the modified delete_mission router endpoint; assert HTTP 200; wait for background task completion; assert AC1: list_objects_v2 for mission S3 prefix returns KeyCount=0 (verifies S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix ran); assert AC2: workflow query returns 0 items (verifies WorkflowRepository.delete_by_mission ran), memo query returns 0 items (verifies MemoRepository.delete_by_mission ran), WeaverPlan query returns 0 items (verifies WeaverPlanRepository.delete_by_mission ran); assert AC3: mock_bedrock_kb.delete_from_kb called with correct DeleteFromKBDocument objects matching mission S3 URIs.

3. Define E2E fixture `project_with_three_missions` that creates a project record, 3 child missions each with S3 files, DynamoDB records (workflows/memos/WeaverPlans), and project-level S3 objects under workspaces/{workspace}/project/{project_id}/ and workspaces/{workspace}/context/{project_id}/.

4. In `test_e2e_project_with_multiple_missions` (AC4-AC5-AC6): issue DELETE /v1/project/{project_id} via the modified delete_project router endpoint; assert HTTP 200; wait for all background tasks; assert AC4: for each of the 3 child missions, verify S3 prefix empty, workflows/memos/WeaverPlans gone from DynamoDB (all verified via CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_project calling cascade_delete_mission per child); assert AC5: project-level S3 prefixes both empty; assert AC6: mock_delete_ocxp_project called once with correct project_id and workspace.

5. In `test_e2e_partial_failure_recovery`: set up a mission with full resource set; configure S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix mock to raise S3 ClientError on first delete call only; issue first DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id}; assert HTTP 200 (no error surfaced); verify partial state (S3 objects remain, DynamoDB records gone); issue second DELETE /v1/mission/{mission_id}; assert HTTP 200; verify full cleanup complete (S3 now empty, all DynamoDB records still gone, no duplicate errors).
6. Record timing of DELETE response and background task completion; assert cascade completes within a reasonable bound (e.g., under 10 seconds for the test fixture sizes).
7. Tag all tests with pytest.mark.e2e.
8. Use a module-level autouse fixture to enforce test workspace isolation and clean up any residual localstack state after each scenario.
constraints: MUST verify all six acceptance criteria (AC1-AC6) across the three E2E scenarios -- each AC must appear in at least one test assertion
MUST create resources through the full lifecycle (fixtures that directly manipulate DynamoDB are acceptable for setup efficiency, but DELETE must go through the HTTP API)
MUST mock Bedrock KB (ContextService.delete_from_kb) since Bedrock is not available in localstack; assert correct call arguments for AC3
MUST assert HTTP 200 immediately after DELETE before waiting for background tasks -- verifying async pattern does not block response
MUST block test assertions until background tasks complete -- use TestClient synchronous mode or an asyncio event loop flush before asserting storage state
MUST use a unique workspace prefix per E2E test run to prevent pollution between test sessions on shared localstack
DECISION: Cascade runs asynchronously in background task (not blocking HTTP response) -- E2E tests must explicitly account for this async gap before issuing storage assertions
DECISION: _delete_ocxp_project() runs as an independent background task -- E2E project test must verify the mock is called as a separate task from CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_project
MUST NOT depend on test execution order -- each E2E scenario is fully self-contained (blocked_by P3.1 and P3.2 ensures integration fixtures are proven before E2E reuse)
edge_cases: Background task has not completed when assertions run -- test must use a synchronous TestClient or explicitly flush the background task queue before asserting
Project with 3 child missions where one mission's cascade fails mid-way -- E2E test verifies the other 2 missions are still cleaned up and the error is logged
WeaverPlan with two versions (v1 and v2 sort keys) -- both versions must be deleted; assert both sort keys are gone from DynamoDB

S3 pagination in E2E: upload more than 1000 S3 objects under a single mission prefix to verify pagination in S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix (or use a smaller threshold mock if 1000 is impractical for E2E)
delete_from_kb called with an empty document list when mission has zero KB-indexed files -- verify no exception raised and AC3 assertion adapts (mock called with empty list or not called)
Second DELETE in partial failure recovery scenario returns 200 even when mission DynamoDB record was already deleted by first attempt -- idempotency must hold at E2E level

spec.name: cascade_delete_final_code_review
spec.summary: Final code review and refactoring pass across all seven files introduced or modified for cascade delete. Enforces Python type annotations, docstrings, no bare except clauses, consistent structured logging field names, and a clean ruff/mypy pass with no new warnings. Reviews CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_mission, CascadeDeleteService.cascade_delete_project, S3Repository.delete_objects_by_prefix, WorkflowRepository.delete_by_mission, MemoRepository.delete_by_mission, WeaverPlanRepository.delete_by_mission, the modified delete_mission endpoint, and the modified delete_project endpoint.
inputs: api/app/services/cascade_delete_service.py: source file -- primary new service containing cascade_delete_mission and cascade_delete_project methods
api/infrastructure/repositories/s3_repository.py: source file -- new delete_objects_by_prefix method added
api/features/core/workflows/repository.py: source file -- new delete_by_mission method added
api/features/core/memos/repository.py: source file -- new delete_by_mission method added
api/features/core/weaver/repository.py: source file -- new delete_by_mission method added
api/features/core/missions/router.py: source file -- delete_mission modified to register cascade_delete_mission as a BackgroundTask
api/features/core/projects/router.py: source file -- delete_project modified to register cascade_delete_project and retain _delete_ocxp_project as independent BackgroundTasks
ruff: linting tool -- run as `ruff check api/` with no new warnings threshold
mypy: type checker -- run as `mypy api/` with no new errors threshold
outputs: void: no code artifact produced -- output is a set of in-place edits to the seven source files
ruff exit code 0 -- zero new ruff warnings introduced by the seven files
mypy exit code 0 -- zero new mypy errors introduced by the seven files
review checklist: documented confirmation that all eight exit criteria are satisfied
behavior: 1. For each of the seven files, scan all public method signatures and verify every parameter and return type has an explicit Python type annotation; add missing annotations using types from the existing codebase (e.g., str, int, list[str], CascadeDeleteResult). Specifically verify: cascade_delete_mission(mission_id: str, workspace: str) -> CascadeDeleteResult, cascade_delete_project(project_id: str, workspace: str) -> CascadeDeleteResult, delete_objects_by_prefix(prefix: str, workspace: str) -> int, delete_by_mission(mission_id: str, workspace: str) -> int for all three repository methods.

2. For each public method in the seven files, verify a docstring is present that describes: what the method does, its parameters, its return value, and any exceptions it raises; add missing docstrings following the existing codebase style (single-line or Google-style multi-line). Specifically confirm docstrings on cascade_delete_mission, cascade_delete_project, delete_objects_by_prefix, and all three delete_by_mission implementations.

3. Scan all except clauses in the seven files; replace any bare `except:` or `except Exception:` with specific exception types (e.g., `except botocore.exceptions.ClientError:`, `except boto3.exceptions.Boto3Error:`); ensure no exception is silently swallowed -- each caught exception must be logged.

4. Audit all structured logging calls in the seven files and verify consistent field names across all log sites: `workspace` (str), `entity_id` (str), `resource_type` (str, one of: 's3', 'workflow', 'memo', 'weaver_plan', 'mission', 'project'), `count_deleted` (int, optional); rename any inconsistent field names to match this schema.
5. Run `ruff check api/` and address any new warnings introduced by the seven files; do not modify pre-existing ruff suppressions in unrelated files.
6. Run `mypy api/` and address any new type errors introduced by the seven files; add inline `# type: ignore` only as a last resort and with a comment explaining why.
7. Verify that CascadeDeleteService is registered correctly in the dependency injection system (Depends() in routers) and that the factory/constructor matches the injected signature.

8. Verify that broadcast_mission_deleted() and broadcast_project_deleted() are still called from the respective routers (not moved into cascade service) -- confirm no behavioral regression from refactoring.

9. Verify that the modified delete_mission router endpoint registers cascade_delete_mission as a BackgroundTask matching the existing _delete_ocxp_project() pattern at router.py:161; verify the modified delete_project router registers cascade_delete_project and _delete_ocxp_project as two independent BackgroundTasks.
10. Document the review completion in a summary comment at the top of cascade_delete_service.py listing the review date, reviewer, and all seven files reviewed.
constraints: MUST NOT introduce any new ruff warnings -- run ruff check before and after to confirm diff is clean
MUST NOT introduce any new mypy errors -- existing mypy baseline errors (if any) are not the scope of this review
MUST replace all bare except clauses with specific exception types -- `except Exception:` is also disallowed unless it re-raises or is the outermost handler in a BackgroundTask
MUST use consistent log field names across all seven files: workspace, entity_id, resource_type, count_deleted -- any deviation is a review failure
MUST verify broadcast calls (broadcast_mission_deleted, broadcast_project_deleted) remain in the router layer, not moved into cascade service
MUST NOT change the public API signatures of any of the seven files -- this is a non-breaking refactoring pass only

MUST verify that all new public methods across the four repository files (delete_objects_by_prefix, and all three delete_by_mission) have both type annotations and docstrings before marking review complete

MUST verify cascade_delete_mission and cascade_delete_project in CascadeDeleteService both have complete type annotations, docstrings, and that every try/except block appends to CascadeDeleteResult.errors and logs the exception before continuing

DECISION: Cascade runs as a BackgroundTask, not blocking HTTP response -- review must verify the BackgroundTask registration pattern in both routers matches the existing _delete_ocxp_project() pattern at router.py:161

DECISION: Individual step failures logged and collected but do not stop cascade -- review must confirm every try/except in cascade_delete_mission and cascade_delete_project appends to CascadeDeleteResult.errors and logs the exception before continuing
edge_cases: A repository method that only has a private underscore-prefixed name -- private methods do not require docstrings per the review scope, but any method called from cascade_delete_service.py must be treated as effectively public

ruff auto-fix changes the formatting of a method in an unrelated section of a file -- accept formatting changes but verify no logic changes were introduced by auto-fix
A structured log call uses positional args instead of keyword args (e.g., logger.info('deleted %s', count)) -- convert to structured keyword format for consistency
CascadeDeleteResult dataclass or TypedDict is missing a field type annotation -- add the annotation during review pass
broadcast_mission_deleted is called with wrong argument order after refactoring -- verify argument order matches broadcast_service.py:206 signature
delete_objects_by_prefix has an untyped pagination loop variable -- add explicit type annotation to the continuation token variable to satisfy mypy
One of the three delete_by_mission implementations catches a generic Exception instead of ClientError -- replace with botocore.exceptions.ClientError during review
